input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Good — both .NET 9 and .NET 10 runtimes are available. I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project targeting both TFMs.
data.reasoningText:
Now I need to set up a BenchmarkDotNet project that targets both net9.0 and net10.0, benchmarking the three methods: AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount.

Let me create a benchmark project that:
1. References the TextAnalyzer source
2. Multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0
3. Benchmarks the three methods

I'll create a separate benchmark project directory.

toolCalls.name: create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-87d28a7d745145509f82a917bca65e7a/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-87d28a7d745145509f82a917bca65e7a/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-87d28a7d745145509f82a917bca65e7a/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,13 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.*" />
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version installed doesn't have built-in support for .NET 10. I need to check what version is available and use the custom moniker approach.

Let me use `CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion` or similar approach, or just use the moniker string directly.
